seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with AG2 + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with AG2 + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Workflow Automation with AG2 enables sophisticated multi-agent systems for multi-step task execution, API integrations, and human-in-the-loop approvals. Guardrails are essential to prevent conversation drift, ensure action correctness, and validate permissions in these complex workflows. Vex provides runtime verification to maintain reliability and safety in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with AG2
architectureOverview.description:
AG2's workflow automation typically involves a GroupChatManager orchestrating specialized agents: a Planner agent decomposes tasks, Executor agents handle API calls and conditional logic, and a Reviewer agent manages human approvals. Agents communicate via registered message hooks, enabling real-time monitoring. The architecture supports sequential and parallel execution with dynamic speaker selection for consensus on critical decisions.

API integrations are handled by tool-enabled agents that validate inputs before execution, while human-in-the-loop steps pause workflows for approval via integrated UIs. Conditional branching uses agent reasoning to select paths, with rollback mechanisms implemented through state tracking and compensation actions. Vex integrates as a message hook to verify each step against defined policies.

This setup scales to enterprise workflows but introduces risks around off-topic drift and incorrect consensus, making structured verification critical.

risks.name: Agent conversation spiraling off-topic
risks.description: Planner and executor agents diverge from workflow steps during extended reasoning
risks.mitigation: Vex message hooks enforce topic adherence and step progression checks

risks.name: Group chat consensus on incorrect facts
risks.description: Agents agree on invalid API responses or flawed conditional logic
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies factual accuracy and idempotency before consensus

risks.name: Code execution agents producing harmful outputs
risks.description: Executor agents generate unsafe API calls or destructive rollback actions
risks.mitigation: Vex permission validation blocks unauthorized or harmful actions

risks.name: Speaker selection bias in multi-agent groups
risks.description: Dominant agents bypass human approval or override valid consensus
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors speaker patterns and enforces equitable participation

implementation.title: Basic AG2 Workflow Group Setup
implementation.description: Initialize GroupChat with planner, executor, and reviewer agents for workflow automation
implementation.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from ag2.agent import AssistantAgent

planner = AssistantAgent(name="Planner", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"})
executor = AssistantAgent(name="Executor", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"}, tools=[api_tool])
reviewer = AssistantAgent(name="Reviewer", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"})

groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[planner, executor, reviewer], messages=[], max_round=20)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"})

result = manager.initiate_chat(recipient=planner, message="Process order #12345")

implementation.title: API Integration with Conditional Logic
implementation.description: Executor agent handles API calls with branching based on response validation

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval Handler
implementation.description: Reviewer agent pauses workflow for human approval before critical actions
implementation.code:
def human_approval_gate(proposal: str) -> str:
    print(f"🚨 HUMAN APPROVAL NEEDED: {proposal}")
    approval = input("Approve? (y/n): ").strip().lower()
    return "approved" if approval == 'y' else "rejected"

reviewer_tools = [human_approval_gate]
reviewer = AssistantAgent(
    name="Reviewer",
    system_message="Always request human approval for orders > $1000",
    llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"},
    tools=reviewer_tools
)

implementation.title: Rollback and Idempotency Handler
implementation.description: Implement safe rollback with idempotency checks for failed workflow steps

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Message Hook on GroupChat
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as a message hook to verify all agent communications in real-time
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
from ag2 import GroupChatManager

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

@vex.message_hook()
def verify_workflow_message(sender: str, message: str, context: dict) -> bool:
    policies = [
        "topic:workflow-automation",
        "severity<=medium",
        "no-harmful-actions",
        "human-approval-required"
    ]
    result = vex.verify_message(message, policies=policies, context=context)
    return result.approved

# Register hook with GroupChatManager
manager = GroupChatManager(
    groupchat=groupchat,
    llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"},
    message_hooks=[verify_workflow_message]
)

cta.heading: Secure Your AG2 Workflow Automation
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install ag2 vex-sdk` and register message hooks to protect your production workflows.
